beats.narration: The earth behaves like a magnet, roughly half a gauss underfoot. Magnetic particle testing builds fields thousands of times stronger, then reads where they escape.

payload.title: MAGNETIC FIELDS
payload.sub: Build the field strong — then read where it escapes.

beats.narration: Flux lines follow four rules: closed loops, never crossing, crowding where the field is strong, and favoring steel over air.
Electric current creates the field: circular flux around a straight conductor, and one longitudinal field down the axis of a coil.
Close a magnet into a ring: poles vanish, flux stays inside. Saw a slot, and poles snap back, leaking flux across the cut.

payload.heading: Closed ring vs slotted ring
payload.note: POLES AT THE CUT — FLUX LEAKS ACROSS

beats.narration: A crack is that slot in miniature: cut the flux, poles appear, iron clings. That chain is the entire principle of magnetic particle testing.

payload.sub: The entire principle of magnetic particle testing.
